data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260508T050621-7fdebb5e/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260508T050621-7fdebb5e/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260508T050621-7fdebb5e/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260508T050621-7fdebb5e/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260508T050621-7fdebb5e/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 3. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-2599ce10">
    <title>agent: split implementation, evidence, and landed revision fields</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
`ExecuteBeadResult.ResultRev` is overloaded. Depending on where the caller observes it, the field can mean the worker implementation commit, a merge commit, or the trailing evidence commit. That makes candidate review, landing, replay, server state, and UI drill-down ambiguous.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:26-37 defines `ExecuteBeadResult` with only `BaseRev` and `ResultRev`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1549-1584 mutates `res.ResultRev = land.NewTip`, replacing the original worker result revision with the target branch tip after fast-forward or merge.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1614-1623 builds `LandRequest.ResultRev` from `res.ResultRev`, so callers must know whether the field still names the candidate commit or has already been rewritten.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:756-792 uses `ResultRev` both to decide whether to land and to report the final worker result.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:979-992 persists `WorkerExecutionResult.ResultRev` without distinguishing candidate, landed, or evidence revisions.
- cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:23-75 and 175-182 expose only `result_rev` in execution manifests/results.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add explicit fields for the revision roles, for example `ImplementationRev` or `CandidateRev`, `LandedRev`, and `EvidenceRev`.
- Define `ResultRev` as a backwards-compatible alias during migration only; document which field it mirrors and stop using it for new internal decisions.
- LandRequest construction must use the implementation/candidate revision, never an evidence or already-landed revision.
- ApplyLandResult must preserve the implementation/candidate revision and set landed/evidence fields separately.
- Server worker reports, state persistence, execution manifests, and UI/query loaders must expose the revision triplet so operators can trace implementation, branch landing, and evidence publication independently.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change merge strategy, candidate review policy, or the execute-bead evidence file format beyond additive fields.
- Do not rewrite existing historical execution bundles.
- Do not remove `result_rev` until a later compatibility cleanup.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-31e07fdc. Depends on ddx-7cb3153d so the new semantics line up with candidate refs instead of preserving the ambiguous raw worktree SHA model.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestApplyLandResult_PreservesImplementationRevAndSetsLandedRev proves a successful land keeps the original implementation/candidate revision and records the target branch tip separately.
2. TestBuildLandRequest_UsesImplementationRevNotEvidenceRev proves LandRequest.ResultRev is built from the candidate/implementation revision even after evidence or landed fields are populated.
3. TestExecutionBundle_ExposesRevisionTriplet proves manifest/result loading exposes implementation/candidate, landed, and evidence revisions while preserving `result_rev` compatibility.
4. TestWorkerReport_DistinguishesCandidateAndLandedRev proves server worker state and operator output no longer collapse candidate and landed revision into one field.
5. TestNoChangesRevisionSemantics_PreservesBaseRev verifies no-change attempts do not fabricate landed/evidence revisions and keep base/candidate semantics clear.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... -run "TestApplyLandResult_PreservesImplementationRevAndSetsLandedRev|TestBuildLandRequest_UsesImplementationRevNotEvidenceRev|TestExecutionBundle_ExposesRevisionTriplet|TestWorkerReport_DistinguishesCandidateAndLandedRev|TestNoChangesRevisionSemantics_PreservesBaseRev" -count=1 passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:evidence, area:git, kind:refactor, reliability, adr:024, spec:FEAT-010, candidate-cycle</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-31e07fdc" base-rev="5903531ad3ba479fd5af8f7abbc25c10ad4d6bc3" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260508T050621-7fdebb5e"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All 5 AC-mandated tests pass, working tree is clean, and lefthook pre-commit is green. The commit `084304c9c` (along with the prior checkpoints `4a32e838d` and `b74a2c500`) contains all the changes for bead `ddx-2599ce10`.

**Summary of what was done:**

- **`ExecuteBeadResult`**: Added `ImplementationRev`, `LandedRev`, `EvidenceRev` fields
- **`ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult`**: Before overwriting `ResultRev` with the branch tip, saves the worker commit as `ImplementationRev`; sets `LandedRev = land.NewTip`; keeps `ResultRev` as a backwards-compat alias
- **`BuildLandRequestFromResult`**: Uses `ImplementationRev` over `ResultRev` when set, so a second call after landing always gets the candidate commit
- **`ExecuteBeadReport`**, **`agenttry.Report`**, **adapters**: Triplet propagated through the try layer
- **`WorkerExecutionResult`**: Exposes all three fields separately
- **`executionBundleManifest/result`**, **`loadExecutionBundle`**, **`ddxgraphql.Execution`**: Bundle loading surfaces the triplet to the server API
- **Tests**: 5 AC-mandated tests all pass, plus 4 additional edge-case tests
